items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A documentation gap was identified in the Azure Management C# emitter package (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`): the newly added `client-options.md` reference page (documenting `@clientOption` keys `resource-name-constraint` and `resource-rbac-roles`) is not linked from the top-level README or the `docs/index.mdx`, making these features undiscoverable for spec authors.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` (emitter/generator npm package in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`)
**Affected API:** `@clientOption` decorator keys: `resource-name-constraint`, `resource-rbac-roles`
**Scenarios:**
- README has no mention of `@clientOption`, `resource-name-constraint`, or `resource-rbac-roles`, and no link to `client-options.md`
- `docs/index.mdx` only links to `emitter.md` and not `client-options.md`
- No "Supported decorators" or "Advanced customization" section exists to guide spec authors

**Root ask:** Update `README.md` and `docs/index.mdx` to surface the `client-options.md` reference page and briefly describe the two supported `@clientOption` keys.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no SDK runtime behavior is affected.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Open `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` and verify there is no reference to `@clientOption`, `resource-name-constraint`, or `resource-rbac-roles`
2. Open `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx` and confirm `client-options.md` is not linked
3. Review `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/client-options.md` (added in PR #57924) to understand the content that needs linking
4. Add a new section to README and a new entry in `docs/index.mdx` pointing to `client-options.md`
5. Run `npm install && npm run lint && npm run prettier` from `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` to validate formatting
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The issue is about the Azure Management plane C# emitter (`http-client-csharp-mgmt`), which generates `Azure.ResourceManager.*` SDKs. The `#PRLabel: %CodeGen %Mgmt` CODEOWNERS pattern for `/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` confirms this categorization.
- **Service:** `CodeGen` — Code generation issues for emitters/generators in `/eng/packages/` use the "CodeGen" service label per repository convention.
- **Confidence:** High — The issue explicitly names the package and service directory; no ambiguity between categories or services. Pattern confirmed by `# ServiceLabel: %CodeGen %Mgmt` in CODEOWNERS and similar open issues with matching labels.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %CodeGen %Mgmt` (line 1201) — both "CodeGen" and "Mgmt" labels are predicted; the entry requires both, so it matches.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** live1206, ArcturusZhang, ArthurMa1978
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned live1206 (randomly selected from 3 AzureSdkOwners); all 3 owners mentioned in routing comment.
- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan reached line 1201 (`%CodeGen %Mgmt`) before the less-specific line 1198 (`%CodeGen` only); matched at line 1201 and stopped. No entries after line 1202 matched both CodeGen and Mgmt.
</details>
